prompt.content:
---
module: schemas_attribution
deps: [schemas_line_range]
targets: [rust]
tests:
  - name: a well-formed document with three entries validates
    given: { module: greeting, target: typescript, entries: 3 }
    expect: { entries: 3 }
  - name: an entry missing its file is rejected
    given: { entry: "{promptLines:[1,1], lines:[1,1], note:x}" }
    expect: { accepts: false }
  - name: an entry with an empty file string is rejected
    given: { entry: "{promptLines:[1,1], file:'', lines:[1,1]}" }
    expect: { accepts: false }
  - name: a non-integer line range is rejected
    given: { entry: "{promptLines:[1,1], file:a.ts, lines:[1.5,2]}" }
    expect: { accepts: false }
  - name: a single-line range normalizes to a start-equals-end pair
    given: { entry: "{promptLines:[8], file:a.ts, lines:3}" }
    expect: { promptLines: [8, 8], lines: [3, 3] }
  - name: a missing note defaults to the empty string
    given: { entry: "{promptLines:[1,1], file:a.ts, lines:[1,1]}" }
    expect: { note: "" }
  - name: parsing a non-list of entries yields an empty list
    given: { entries: "{}" }
    expect: { entries: 0 }
  - name: a malformed entry list is rejected
    given: { entries: "[{promptLines:'nope', file:a, lines:[1,2]}]" }
    expect: { accepts: false }
  - name: entries-at-body-line returns every entry whose prompt range contains the line
    given: { body_line: 3 }
    expect: { notes: ["covers trimming", "trims whitespace"] }
---
# The attribution schema: prompt-line to file-line mappings

This module defines the serde types and validators for a NAPL **attribution
document** — the record mapping ranges of a prompt's body lines to the ranges of
generated file lines they produced. It is pure: no I/O. Bring in `serde`,
`serde_json`.

## Where this code lives

The working directory is a Cargo workspace whose root manifest is written and
owned by the toolchain — leave it alone. Create this module as its own member
crate in a subdirectory named `schemas_attribution/`:
`schemas_attribution/Cargo.toml` (package name `schemas_attribution`) and
`schemas_attribution/src/lib.rs`. Touch nothing outside `schemas_attribution/`.
Ensure `cargo test` passes from the workspace root before finishing.

## Builds on the `schemas_line_range` module of this workspace

Every line range in this schema is an inclusive 1-based line range that accepts
several JSON spellings (a bare number `n` meaning `n..=n`, a one-element array
`[n]` meaning `n..=n`, or a two-element array `[a, b]`), with each endpoint
required to be an integer at least 1. That value type — and its lenient
deserializer — is the **`schemas_line_range`** module of this same workspace,
generated as a sibling member crate in the `schemas_line_range/` directory (so
`../schemas_line_range` relative to this crate). It exposes a public type
`LineRange` with public `start` and `end` fields (`u32`), a
`new(start, end)` constructor, equality, copy, and a serde `Deserialize` that
performs exactly the normalization above.

Add a path dependency on that sibling member crate in your `Cargo.toml` (depend
on the `schemas_line_range` crate by path — it lives at `../schemas_line_range`)
and use its `LineRange` for every line-range field below. Do **not** reimplement
line-range parsing here, and do not depend on any hand-written crate — depend
only on the generated sibling `schemas_line_range` member crate. This sibling
path dependency is part of the same workspace, not an external crates.io
dependency, so add it even though the general guidance is to avoid outside
dependencies.

## The types

Expose these public types:

- `AttributionEntry`: one mapping entry, deserializable, comparable, cloneable,
  with public fields:
  - `prompt_lines: LineRange` whose JSON key is **`promptLines`** (camelCase).
  - `file: String`.
  - `lines: LineRange`.
  - `note: String` defaulting to the empty string when the key is absent.
- `Attribution`: a whole document, deserializable, comparable, cloneable, with
  public fields:
  - `module: String`.
  - `target: String`.
  - `entries: Vec<AttributionEntry>` defaulting to empty when absent.

## Validation

- `validate_attribution(value: serde_json::Value) -> Result<Attribution, _>`:
  deserialize the JSON value into an `Attribution`, then validate it. `module`
  must not be empty, `target` must not be empty, and every entry's `file` must
  not be empty. A structurally malformed value (for example a line range that is
  not an integer, such as `1.5`) is a deserialization error. An entry missing
  its `file`, or with an empty `file`, is rejected. Rejection is an error, not a
  panic.
- `parse_attribution_entries(value: serde_json::Value) -> Result<Vec<AttributionEntry>, _>`:
  if the value is **not** a JSON array, return an empty list (no error).
  Otherwise deserialize it as a list of entries and validate each entry's
  non-empty `file`. A malformed list (an entry whose `promptLines` is a string,
  say) is an error.

Because line ranges deserialize leniently, a single-line spelling normalizes:
an entry with `"promptLines": [8]` and `"lines": 3` yields `prompt_lines`
`8..=8` and `lines` `3..=3`. A missing `note` yields the empty string.

## Lookup

- `entries_at_body_line(attribution: &Attribution, body_line: u32) -> Vec<&AttributionEntry>`:
  return references to every entry whose `promptLines` range contains
  `body_line` (inclusive on both ends), in document order.

For example, given a document whose entries are
`{promptLines:[2,2], note:"builds the greeting"}`,
`{promptLines:[3,4], note:"trims whitespace"}`, and
`{promptLines:[3,3], note:"covers trimming"}`, the entries at body line 3 are the
two whose ranges contain 3 ("trims whitespace" and "covers trimming"), body line
2 yields only "builds the greeting", and body line 9 yields none.

files.content:
[package]
name = "schemas_attribution"
version = "0.1.0"
edition = "2021"

[dependencies]
serde = { version = "1", features = ["derive"] }
serde_json = "1"
schemas_line_range = { path = "../schemas_line_range" }

[dev-dependencies]
serde_json = "1"

attribution.note: AttributionEntry struct with promptLines camelCase rename
Attribution struct with module, target, entries default
validate_attribution deserializes then checks non-empty module/target/file
AttributionError enum and Display impl for rejection errors
parse_attribution_entries returns empty for non-array else validates entries
test for single-line spelling normalization and missing note default
entries_at_body_line filters by inclusive range in document order
sample_document fixture matching the example entries
test asserting entries at body lines 3, 2, 9 match the example
test rejects malformed line range as deserialization error
tests parsing valid list, empty file rejection, malformed list error
test entries default to empty when absent
tests rejects empty module and empty target

files.schemas_attribution.napl:
---
module: schemas_attribution
deps: [schemas_line_range]
targets: [rust]
tests:
  - name: a well-formed document with three entries validates
    given: { module: greeting, target: typescript, entries: 3 }
    expect: { entries: 3 }
  - name: an entry missing its file is rejected
    given: { entry: "{promptLines:[1,1], lines:[1,1], note:x}" }
    expect: { accepts: false }
  - name: an entry with an empty file string is rejected
    given: { entry: "{promptLines:[1,1], file:'', lines:[1,1]}" }
    expect: { accepts: false }
  - name: a non-integer line range is rejected
    given: { entry: "{promptLines:[1,1], file:a.ts, lines:[1.5,2]}" }
    expect: { accepts: false }
  - name: a single-line range normalizes to a start-equals-end pair
    given: { entry: "{promptLines:[8], file:a.ts, lines:3}" }
    expect: { promptLines: [8, 8], lines: [3, 3] }
  - name: a missing note defaults to the empty string
    given: { entry: "{promptLines:[1,1], file:a.ts, lines:[1,1]}" }
    expect: { note: "" }
  - name: parsing a non-list of entries yields an empty list
    given: { entries: "{}" }
    expect: { entries: 0 }
  - name: a malformed entry list is rejected
    given: { entries: "[{promptLines:'nope', file:a, lines:[1,2]}]" }
    expect: { accepts: false }
  - name: entries-at-body-line returns every entry whose prompt range contains the line
    given: { body_line: 3 }
    expect: { notes: ["covers trimming", "trims whitespace"] }
---
# The attribution schema: prompt-line to file-line mappings

This module defines the serde types and validators for a NAPL **attribution

Add a path dependency on that sibling member crate in your `Cargo.toml` (depend

## The types

Expose these public types:

- `AttributionEntry`: one mapping entry, deserializable, comparable, cloneable,

  - `lines: LineRange`.

- `entries_at_body_line(attribution: &Attribution, body_line: u32) -> Vec<&AttributionEntry>`:
  return references to every entry whose `promptLines` range contains

For example, given a document whose entries are
